key_insight: GutCore is a foundation model that uses whole endoscopy examinations, not just single frames, to assess gastric cancer presence, invasion depth, biomarkers, and prognosis at the patient level.
clinical_relevance: This approach could help endoscopy teams better triage gastric cancer cases and estimate risk from routine stored images, but it still needs external validation before clinical use.
method: Pretrained on 5.6 million de-identified endoscopic images from more than 10 hospitals.
Evaluated on 11,035 internal endoscopic examinations with cancer and benign control cases.
Aggregated all images from each exam to predict cancer status, invasion depth, biomarkers, and survival risk.
evidence_signal: GutCore achieved very high patient-level AUCs for cancer detection (0.995) and muscularis propria invasion (0.960), and it separated survival in advanced gastric cancer with a log-rank P < .0001.
watch_item: Performance varied by biomarker and the study was retrospective, so generalizability and real-world workflow impact remain unproven.
paper_title: GutCore: An Endoscopy Foundation Model for Whole-Case Gastric Cancer Analysis
